My customer demands the supplier tax ID to be shown here. Their warehouse is maintained as a supplier in the system.

entry.title: Re: AI Developer Challenge - Week 1: Grounding with SAP Generative AI Hub

Successfully wrapped up Week 1 challenge — great start!

User ID: GE304679

entry.title: Re: Is "free" course completion viewable to LH admins for...
entry.content:
Hi @amysumma,

The SAP Learning Hub analytics dashboard shows learning activity data only for users who hold a valid SAP Learning Hub license.

With that in mind, here is how it applies to your scenario:

For your 300 licensed learners (SAP Learning Hub license holders): Yes, the dashboard shows their learning activity regardless of whether the course was free or subscription-based. As long as the learner has a SAP Learning Hub license, their progress is visible to your learning analysts.

For your 200 unlicensed learners (free content only): As learning activity in the SAP Learning Hub analytics dashboard is available for SAP Learning Hub license holders only, users accessing free content without a license are not included in the dashboard reporting.

As a practical path forward: if visibility into those 200 learners' progress is important for your program, the simplest option would be to extend Learning Hub licenses to cover them.

entry.title: Re: Introducing My Metrics: Your Personalized view of Metrics in SAP Analytics Cloud
entry.content:
Hello @N1kh1l,

I agree and it's a pity that only standard color palette is used in My Metrics. There exists an influence item having at least the same colors as in the source story. It has currently only 8 votes. 😢

entry.title: How SAP Joule is Transforming the Developer Experience
entry.content:
In today’s rapidly evolving tech landscape, being an ABAP developer is no longer just about writing code. To stay relevant and competitive, developers must embrace AI-powered tools that enhance productivity, improve code quality, and accelerate delivery.

One such innovation is SAP Joule, an AI assistant designed specifically for SAP developers. Integrated directly into ABAP Development Tools for Eclipse, Joule brings generative AI capabilities into your daily development workflow - without requiring you to switch between tools.

Let’s explore how Joule is transforming ABAP development.

What is SAP Joule for Developers?

SAP Joule is an AI-powered assistant that supports developers throughout the entire development lifecycle - from writing and understanding code to testing, optimization, and even migration.

Think of it as a smart coding partner that understands your context and helps you work faster and smarter.

 

Key features of Joule for Developers :

   1. Chat and Help feature    

Joule Chat allows you to interact using simple, natural language.

  • Ask questions about ABAP concepts
  • Get instant help from SAP documentation
  • Retrieve context-aware answers based on your work

You can also use commands like:

  • /help > for assistance
  • /explain > to understand code or objects

                    

   2. Code Understanding and Assistance 

Understanding legacy or complex code is often time-consuming. Joule simplifies this with its Explain feature.

It provides clear explanations for:

  • ABAP Classes
  • ABAP Programs
  • ATC findings
  • CDS artifacts

 Example;

/explain _view_name​

        Alternatively, Right-click on objects > Joule > Explain        

 

   3. Predictive Code Completion

Joule enhances your coding speed with AI-driven suggestions.

  • Auto-completes code intelligently
  • Works for classes, interfaces, and programs
  • Accept suggestions instantly using Tab

You can even write a requirement as a comment, and Joule will generate the code for you as ghost text.

       Settings 
Go to Window > Preferences > Source Code Editors > Code Completion, then review or update your settings. Make sure the "Trigger predictive code completion" option is enabled.

 

To display predictive suggestions in a dialog optimized for screen readers, go to Window > Preferences > ABAP Development > Accessibility > Predictive Code Completion.

You can manually trigger suggestions using Alt + J (Windows) or Option + J (Mac). Automatic triggering can also be turned on or off through the search dialog (Ctrl + 3).

 

 

To display proposals in a dialog box, enable the "Show Proposals in a dialog box" option.

 

To receive a suggested solution, write your requirement in plain commented text. The proposed solution will appear as ghost text. Press Tab to accept it.

 

4. Testing Support 

Writing unit tests becomes significantly easier with Joule.

  • Automatically generates ABAP Unit tests
  • Supports global and local classes
  • Generates test classes for CDS entities

This improves:

  • Test coverage
  • Code reliability
  • Developer productivity

To create an ABAP Unit test, right-click on the class and choose Joule > Start Chat for ABAP Unit. The Joule perspective will then present recommended next steps.

     
 
  
 

In Joule Chat, click "Add Tests to New Test Class".

                     
 
Joule will suggest ABAP Unit tests, giving you options to Apply, Insert, Copy, Refactor, or Generate Test.
 
 
 

  5. Development  

Joule can help you build applications faster by generating key components.

RAP Business Logic

  • Generates determinations and validations
  • Uses simple descriptions to create logic

 

Enter the requirement and specify what each method is expected to perform.

The proposal is generated based on your method description, and you can modify it manually if needed.

OData UI Services

  • Create complete applications from scratch
  • Automatically generates:
    • Service definitions
    • CDS views
    • Behavior definitions
    • Database tables

This significantly reduces manual effort in building applications.

Steps to create an OData UI application:

  1. Right-click on the package and select Generate ABAP Repository Objects.

     2. Choose OData UI Service from Scratch.          

         3. In the Configure Generator window, enter a description of your BO in Joule Chat. For example:           

        4.   Based on your prompt, Joule will suggest the BO entities and fields. If they match your requirements, click Accept. You can also modify them manually or with Joule’s assistance. Once satisfied, click Next.              

 

        5. Review the list of artifacts to be generated, then click Next. Select the Transport Request and click Finish.                    

 

Consume OData

The Consume capability in Joule lets you generate OData requests and corresponding ABAP code via the OData Client Proxy.
You need a service consumption model with a model class, created in ABAP Development Tools for Eclipse using the service metadata.

Steps:

  • Open the service consumption model of your OData service.
  • Start Joule chat:
    • Right-click an object > Joule > Consume, or
    • Open Joule chat and type /consume.

How It Works

  • Known OData request: Enter it directly (e.g., GET /Travel?$select=ID) and Joule will generate the corresponding ABAP code.
  • Need help creating a request: Describe your goal in plain language, and Joule will generate both the OData request and ABAP code.

You can adjust the generated request or code and try again.

 

  6. Migration  

Joule migration assistant helps you:

  • Understand ATC findings
  • Identify obsolete or incompatible code
  • Adapt to data model changes

This reduces risk and speeds up migration projects.

 

SAP Joule is more than just a development tool - it’s a game changer for ABAP developers. By integrating AI into everyday workflows, it helps developers write cleaner code, automate repetitive tasks, and quickly understand complex business objects.

entry.title: Re: AI Developer Challenge - Week 1: Grounding with SAP Generative AI Hub
Comparison of Read Indicators and Read Indicator Flexibility in SAP DM
entry.content:
Hi Team, 

We are currently working on a use case in SAP Digital Manufacturing (DM) where we have designed a Production Process Definition (PPD) to fetch machine tag values from shop floor systems (e.g., OPC UA). 

As part of our implementation, we are using both "Read Indicators" and "Read Indicators Flexibly" services. However, we have observed the following limitations:

  1. In Read Indicators, all indicator parameters (tags) must be pre-configured within the service, making it static in nature.
  2. In Read Indicators Flexibly, although it provides more flexibility, it still requires input parameters such as asset, reference name, and structure path, which limits our ability to make the solution fully dynamic for all tag values.

This restricts flexibility, as:

  1. We are unable to pass indicator/tag parameters dynamically at runtime.
  2. Any addition of new indicators in the shop floor system requires manual updates to the PPD design. 

We would like clarification on the following: 

  1. Which service (standard vs flexible) is recommended to achieve fully dynamic indicator/tag retrieval without requiring design-time configuration?
  2. Are there any best practices or alternative approaches to handle dynamic addition of new shop floor indicators without modifying the PPD? 

Our goal is to design a scalable and dynamic solution where new machine tags can be consumed without frequent design changes. 

Kindly provide guidance on the recommended approach

Thanks 🙂

entry.title: Re: SAP Sustainability Footprint Management: Q1-26 Updates & Highlights
entry.content: Thanks for the great summaries each quarter.

entry.title: What’s New in Q1-2026? SAP Sustainability Footprint Management Updates
entry.content:
To stay ahead in the world of ESG, your tools need to evolve as fast as the regulations do. SAP has just released the Q1-2026 highlights for SAP Sustainability Footprint Management, and there are some game-changing features for anyone managing carbon data.

The latest updates focus on precision, automation, and expanding the scope of audit-ready calculations.

Key Highlights from Q1-2026: 🚛 Enhanced Transport Footprints: New capabilities for customer-relevant transport emissions, including better distance control and route automation. 📊 New Analytics App: A dedicated "Transport" analytics app providing a map-based view of your corporate and product footprints. 🎯 Granular Scope 3 Reporting: Improved attribution for purchased product footprints, ensuring a more accurate split between Scope 3.1 and 3.2. 🤖 Increased Automation: New usability enhancements in data collection forms and manual imports to reduce the "manual heavy lifting" of ESG data.

Whether you are deep into implementation or just planning your roadmap, these updates show exactly how SAP is turning sustainability from a reporting burden into a strategic advantage.

👉 I highly recommend checking out the full article for all the details: https://community.sap.com/t5/supply-chain-management-blog-posts-by-sap/sap-sustainability-footprint-management-q1-26-updates-amp-highlights/ba-p/14363686

#SAP #Sustainability #FootprintManagement #Q12026 #CarbonAccounting #ESGUpdates #SupplyChain #NetZero #SAPCommunity

entry.title: Re: CAP, RAP, or Both: Stop Hiding Behind 'It Depends'
entry.content: @walteraguerom , a most informative article that goes beyond the superficial argument that "it depends". Brilliant! I remember a customer remarking that they wouldn't RAP using ABAP Cloud because the service costs were too high, even though they had a team of experienced ABAP developers. It would be CAP every time. I didn't delve deeper into this remark, as I was only there for a short while. I would be interested in your thoughts, though, if I reread your article carefully, the answer may already be there. Thanks.

entry.title: Re: ABAP RAP: Defaulting action parameters with complex business logic in a Fiori Elements applicati
entry.content: Thanks for the blog! This is a common requirement, to enable the user with custom prefilled values.

entry.title: Migration tool for your SAP Build Apps frontend web applications
entry.content:
In my former blog post, I announced that SAP Build Apps will be deprecated, and today I’m happy to share that a migration tool for your frontend web application built with SAP Build Apps is ready to support you with your transition needs. Previously, when you wanted to transition from SAP Build Apps frontend web application offering to SAP Build tools, you hit a brick wall. You were left with a proprietary .mtar export file and a harsh realization: migrating meant essentially rebuilding your entire application from scratch.

Over the past period, we’ve received a tremendous amount of great, honest, and passionate feedback from our customers, partners, and community. Our proposed migration path of the backend is working, but the recreation of the web application frontend was not acceptable. And this is why we focused all our efforts on creating a transformer tool to support your migration needs. We believe in developer empowerment and digital freedom. Your data, your logic, and your UI designs belong to you. Your software should scale with your ambition, not be constrained by the platform you started on. With SAP Build, you have the right choice.

Today, I am incredibly proud to introduce a migration tool to transform your .mtar file of your frontend web apps into React or SAP UI5. This transformation tool is an automated engine designed to do the heavy lifting of application migration. It takes your raw, exported .mtar archives, securely processes the proprietary configurations, and intelligently translates them into clean and standardized frontend projects which you can easily enhance leveraging your SAP Build environment like SAP Business Application Studio.
Instead of spending weeks manually reverse-engineering views, layouts, and navigation logic, the transformation tool delivers a fully structured React (Vite) or SAP UI5 project.

Starting today, customers of our SAP Build Apps standard offering which are affected by the deprecation can reach out to us directly with the ask to migrate their SAP Build Apps frontend web app into either React or SAP UI5. The process is described here. With React you also have the chance to leverage the SAP Sales & Service Cloud V2 design system (colors, typography, BEM components). Your SAP contact partner is also equipped and can help you navigate if you are currently on an implementation project. Certainly, partners can also reach out to us to realize their clients’ projects.

 

Let’s Build the Future Together

To all the developers, architects, and IT leaders who shared their frustrations and their visions with us: Thank you! Your feedback was the catalyst for this innovation. The transformer isn’t just a conversion tool, it’s our commitment to supporting your growth at every stage of the software lifecycle.

We can't wait to see what you build next!

And finally, stay tuned! SAPPHIRE is around the corner!

entry.title: Register Now: SAP SuccessFactors Roadmap Update | 1H 2026
entry.content:
Curious where SAP SuccessFactors is headed in 2026 and beyond? Join this virtual session for a strategic roadmap update and hear directly from the SAP SuccessFactors team on priorities, direction, and long‑term vision.

What to expect:

  • A high‑level view of the product roadmap and overall strategy
  • Insights into what’s shaping the future of SuccessFactors
  • Global live sessions with automatic captioning and multi-language translations

Sessions are available for partners (April 14) and customers (April 23) across APJ, EMEA, and the Americas.

View session details and register here:  VIP Virtual Program – Strategic Product Roadmap Up... - SAP Community

entry.title: Re: India Localization: Blanket POs for Multi-site Scenarios
entry.content:
Hi @SwapnilKagalkar,
My friend the tension between Blanket/Framework POs, multi‑plant procurement, GST place‑of‑supply rules, and CO object assignment is extremely common in India, especially for companies executing large multi‑state projects.

Below is a consolidated view of how real EPC and industrial companies in India handle this, with patterns that avoid “One Plant, One PO” while still staying compliant and manageable. This is the most widely used model in large EPC firms such as L&T, Tata Projects, BHEL, and many process‑industry companies.

A central/HO purchasing team creates a central Blanket PO (often with a dummy plant or a service plant). The PO does NOT carry GST‑relevant plant information. Actual consumption happens through plant‑specific call‑off POs or service entry sheets. Each plant posts its own GR/SES + invoice, ensuring the correct Place of Supply for GST and correct CO object assignment (WBS/Network/Cost Center) with no cross‑state tax complications.

The reason to chose this is to keep vendor negotiation centralized, Avoids dozens of POs for the same contract and maintains GST correctness at the time of invoice posting

entry.title: Re: SAP Build Apps Deprecation and The Path Forward
entry.content: Hi, again, thank you very much for all your valuable feedback! I tried to reach out to each of you. If not, please do not hesitate to reach out to me. Please also notice that we just released a migration option for frontend web applications built with SAP Build Apps. You can find my blog post here. Looking very forward to hear from you. Best regards, Patrick

entry.title: Blending Model in ODE not as expected
entry.content:
During the migration of our stories from Classic Mode to the Optimized Design Experience (ODE), we have encountered an issue regarding the behavior of model blending.

Initial Situation (Classic)

In the Classic Mode, it was possible to blend multiple models without requiring the linked dimensions to be part of the table layout (rows/columns).
This allowed for a clean and business-relevant data presentation.

Behavior in ODE

Since using blending in the ODE, we observe the following behavior:

  • Linked dimensions (e.g., Object ID, User ID) must be included in the table drill (rows/columns) for the blending to work correctly.
  • If these dimensions are not included, data is either not displayed or the blending does not behave as expected.

Problem Description

This leads to several limitations:

  • The affected dimensions are not relevant for business display purposes and are intentionally not meant to be shown.
  • Forcing these dimensions into the table results in:
    • unnecessarily complex and overloaded tables
    • reduced readability
    • <

entry.title: Print Handling Unit Label During Creation of Inbound Delivery

A new parameter "Process" should be available in the Output Parameter Determination App. After several tests, I was not able to find such parameter. Do you know how to solve the issue? Thanks!

entry.title: Issues in updating standard value from DM to S4
entry.content:
Hi Team,

We are trying to achieve simultaneous group functionality in DM using the order processing mode(Local Routing ERP BOM with Quantity split on steps, without Quantity split on steps).


Example: In SAP DM, we created a local Master Routing with five(5) operations and specified the ERP operation activities (0010…0050) in the reporting steps, maintained the standard values also. We are able to post yield back to S4 from DM, but we are facing issues when posting activity confirmations back to S4.

The error message in Integration Message Dashboard:" Collaboration execution failed due to the external error: 00000000000000000001 confirmations are incorrect. For details see parameter DETAIL_RETURN; Sequence NaN is not defined in order (order number), check entry."

Kindly help us to resolve this issue.

Thanks in advance 🙂

entry.title: HTTPURLLOC demystified
entry.content:
Bringing back the article from SCN which is referenced in the SAP Security Baseline Template. It was initially

Posted by Samuli Kaski in SAP NetWeaver Application Server on Jun 4, 2014 1:25:01 AM. 

The content from that post follows after the jump …

 

Recently I had to configure table HTTPURLLOC in order to support a scenario where users were either accessing the system externally through a hardware reverse proxy connected to SAP Web Dispatcher or the SAP system directly. In this blog I want to share my experience with SCN members.

 

The first take away from this blog is that HTTPURLLOC can't be used for switching from one protocol to another nor rewriting URLs, that happens elsewhere (e.g. Web Dispatcher modification rules, portal configuration, ICF node settings, etc). HTTPURLLOC has values with valid protocol, host and port combinations that can be used to generate URLs, otherwise the generic URL generation of the system is used. That is why HTTPURLLOC is called an exception table, not a URL generation table.

 

So now how does it work? Basically the system looks at the request object and tries to determine the requested host, this is usually the virtual host name, DNS alias, etc. which was requested by the client, e.g. the browser. This is the second take away. The host as requested by the client should be preserved throughout the request, even if there are other proxies, load balancers, etc. in the loop. If the client browser requests abc.company.com, this host should be visible in the request object of the SAP system. The only way to guarantee it is that all appliances/programs are instructed to preserve the host that was requested by the client. Apache has the PreserveProxyHost directive for it, Web Dispatcher does it automatically, others might require further configuration.

 

The third take away is that as with the host requested by the client, the protocol used by the client must be preserved as well. This is especially important if HTTPS is terminated before the request hits the SAP system. Again, configuration directives might be required to insure this. Apache has the RequestHeader directive which can be used to set variable clientprotocol to either HTTPS or HTTP depending on the scenario. Web Dispatcher does it automatically assuming wdisp/add_client_protocol_header is set to true, others might require further configuration.

 

The fourth take away is to understand how entries in HTTPURLLOC are matched against the request. The most important variable is the protocol, second most important variable is the host, third most important variable is the application and last one is the domain. If the client requested abc.company.com with protocol HTTPS and assuming that the host and protocol are preserved meaning the SAP system sees the original values, the HTTPURLLOC table is queried first for all entries that have the protocol HTTPS. Next the requested application is checked, then the domain and finally the host. There are several rules which will affect the end result meaning whether the entry in HTTPURLLOC will be used or not, the best way is to figure it out yourself by debugging method IF_HTTP_SERVER~GET_LOCATION_EXCEPTION of ABAP class CL_HTTP_SERVER.

 

The fifth take away is to understand that HTTPURLLOC is client specific and especially if you are using System Logon, you will have to configure HTTPURLLOC for client 000 as well as the clients you use.

 

The sixth and last take away is to understand that in case the requested host is unknown (it is possible in some scenarios), HTTPURLLOC will be queried based on the protocol and the first match will be used (in ascending order, based on SORT_KEY).

 

Update 11/22/14: in case you want to generate URLs in an offline scenario (background job, classic GUI transaction, basically anything where there is no IF_HTTP_SERVER object), you should create a default entry in HTTPURLLOC and set the SORT_KEY value to the smallest value of all entries. For example, if you want to generate all NWBC URLs with specific protocol, port and host values, create an entry with the lowest SORT_KEY value and set the application to /NWBC/. That way, regardless in which context the NWBC URL is generated, the URL to NWBC will be generated using the values you specified with the lowest SORT_KEY. The key here is to set a value for APPLICATION.

 

I hope this blog will save others time and headaches trying to figure out the mysteries of HTTPURLLOC. See also the official documentation on URL Generation in an AS-ABAP - Web Dispatcher Configuration.

entry.title: SAP Service and Asset Manager mandatory and Hide fields in Create Maintenance Notification Page
entry.content:
Hi experts!

 

I have a business requirement in the Create Maintenance Notification Page, turn mandatory the filling of fields MainWorkCerter, Planner Group and the Catalogs. 

I would like to hide some fields that i'm not using as detection method catalog and Minor work button. 

Also, i would like to change the name of Failure group mode field to a custom lable. 

 

First, is it possible to do this if i configure the Omdo Model and deactivate the fields for example? Or is this just possible, customizing the MDK? 

Best Regards,

Rafael Eij Umeda

SAP Service and Asset Manager 

mobile development kit client

entry.title: Re: AI Developer Challenge - Week 1: Grounding with SAP Generative AI Hub
entry.content: Did you by any chance select the default identity provider when setting up cloud foundry? Could you please delete your dev space, clear your cache and cookies and try again? It is really important that you follow all the Cloud Foundry setup steps exactly as described in the workbook. And let me know whether that fixed it!

entry.title: Re: Elevate your financials using Advance Foreign Currency Valuation (FINS_FXV) in S/4 HANA 2022 OP
entry.content:

Did you get any response your question on consideration for AR migration with Adv. FCV while legacy is using classic FCV.

Thanks

entry.title: Re: Committed date is past in sto in s4 hana
entry.content:
It seems, your system is activated for Backward scheduling. In backward scheduling, the system starts from the requested delivery date (RDD) and works backward through transportation, loading, and production lead times. If material is available earlier, the MAD/commit date can fall before “today.”

To ensure commit dates are never in the past in S/4HANA, switch to forward scheduling or implement a BAdI/user exit that resets past dates to today. This is a configuration/enhancement requirement, not a bug.

entry.title: ServiceNow being an external catalog for SAP Ariba Buying
entry.content:
Hi All!! 

Just wanted to ask if you ever met a scenario where ServiceNow catalog is an external catalog for SAP Ariba Buying?

Few words of explanation. 
A Customer implemented SAP Ariba Buying and now is asking about a scenario of looping in ServiceNow catalog where IT services / items PRs would be created and sent to SAP Ariba to underlay regular Ariba approvals WFs.

In my opinion having Guided Buying as an user front end there is no reason to start the process in ServiceNow - but there is a sine-qua-non condition - ServiceNow has to meet SAP Ariba requirements for being an external catalog.

I am aware of the requirements supplier catalog should meet to be published as an external one but in addition I am struggling with finding a green light / red light answer on if ServiceNow catalog meets this criteria.

I thought someone of you guys met such requirement earlier and knows the yes / no answer 🙂  

Before you ask - yes, I informed a Customer about such eventuality and yes, asked Customer to discuss it with ServiceNow 😉 Just thought our Community is quicker in answering 😉 

Thank you in advance. 
Marcin

entry.title: Integrating Service Now tool with SAP SuccessFactors to pull latest Performance rating
entry.content:
Hi Team,

We are building a new Interface wherein we are integrating Service Now tool with SAP SuccessFactors to pull latest Performance rating to Performance Form.

There is a process we follow where we provide latest rating (after Calibration is over in PM form in SF) in Form under Service now. After the approval of this form, we want to pull this new rating to Performance form.

We have collated the API in this regards.

We are pulling below mentioned fields during establishing this connectivity:

User ID

Form ID

 Old Rating

New Rating 

Rating Scale

Modified By Modified Date

We seek additional information on this which includes:

  1. The API details from SuccessFactors perspective
  2. If any other customer has already performed this connectivity
  3. Any issues do they face
  4. Any direct link from SAP Community who had this implementation would be of good help

Kindly advice if any use case in this regards.

It will be of good help while we implement this new change.

Warm Regards,

Rooppa Khandelwaal

entry.title: Re: Assistance Required for 3% Non-Creditable Input Tax C...
entry.content:
In S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, you cannot create new tax condition types directly in FI customizing — that’s why the system blocked you. Public Cloud restricts deep customizing to preserve compliance and upgrade stability.

So what you can do is you can create new tax codes in the Define Tax Codes for Sales and Purchases configuration activity (SSCUI). When defining the tax code, you can mark it as non‑deductible. This ensures the tax amount is posted to the expense or inventory account rather than to input tax (recoverable VAT/GST). For materials, if the tax is non‑deductible, the system adds it to the material cost (inventory valuation). This is standard behavior

entry.title: Re: Strategies for Downtime-Optimized DMO : doDMO, doC, DMOVE2S4 for large databases
entry.content:
Thanks for this article.

Can we use DMOVE2S4 approach with DMO with system move as well?

entry.title: Re: Recursive BOM: CK739 and CK740 error in CK11N for Rec...
entry.content: Two possible root causes are there for these issues and Resolution is provided in SAP note 3312852

entry.title: Switzerland: Update on Mileage Reimbursement Rates in SAP Business ByDesign
entry.content:
The objective of this blog post is to explain the recent update to Switzerland mileage reimbursement rates announced by the local tax authorities as part of professional expense regulations.

This short guide will help SAP Business ByDesign customers understand the impact and maintain the updated mileage reimbursement rates in the system.

Swiss tax authorities have amended the mileage reimbursement rate for the business use of private vehicles under professional expense regulations. The updated rate is applicable from 1 January 2026 and this change impacts mileage reimbursements reported as professional expenses.

To ensure compliance, users of SAP Business Bydesign are required to review local tax guidance and update their internal expense policies accordingly.

SAP Business ByDesign system provides a configurable option that allows customers to maintain such statutory mileage reimbursement rates for Switzerland in Business Configuration. This enables users to manage mileage reimbursement rates, define validity periods and maintain company-specific car allowance policies where appliable.

Steps to Update Mileage Reimbursement Rates

To reflect the updated mileage reimbursement rates, customers can maintain the configuration as follows:

  1. Navigate to Business Configuration > Overview Search for the activity > Mileage Rates - Switzerland
  2. Open Edit mileage reimbursement rates
  3. Select the 'Statutory Rates' tab > Click Add Row > Maintain new flat mileage reimbursement rate of CHF 0.75/km.
  4. If an updated company specific annual car allowance policy is applicable > maintain it under 'Company-specific rates'
  5. Click Save to activate the changes

By updating the mileage reimbursement configuration, SAP Business ByDesign customers in Switzerland can ensure compliance with local regulations while maintaining accurate and auditable expense reporting.

Legal Source:
https://www.fedlex.admin.ch/eli/oc/2025/578/de

https://www.ssk-csi.ch/de/themen/spesen

Thanks & Regards

Anusha

entry.title: Re: AI Developer Challenge - Week 1: Grounding with SAP Generative AI Hub

entry.title: Re: ABAP test cockpit (ATC) recommendations for governance of clean core ABAP development
entry.content: HI, we have set up the BTP ATC and using the ABAP_CLEAN_CORE_DEVELOPMENT variant but the question is since BTP ABAP variant is higher version compare with our backend. So can it miss identifying the non clean core developments if the BAPI is released in higher versions where as in our version if its not released yet. i dont see any option to set the version of our backend within BTP ATC Configuration. how do we ensure the clean core ATC checks are performed based on our release of SAP system.

entry.title: Re: Project Systems How to Change a WBS element after it...
entry.content: Thank you so much for your quick response - we will try these things.

entry.title: Re: E-Mail Address is not deleted/updated in Cloud ERP fr...
entry.content:
ERP business partner master data supports multiple addresses and communication methods. The integration content is designed to add new values rather than overwrite or delete existing ones, to avoid accidental data loss. Sales Cloud V2 only holds one email, but ERP allows multiple, so the integration is “additive.”

There is no standard delivered option to automatically delete or overwrite old emails in ERP when Sales Cloud V2 changes them. The behavior you see is expected. To change it, you need either:

  • Custom iFlow mapping in SAP Cloud Integration, or
  • ERP BAdI enhancement to enforce “single email only.”

entry.title: Re: SAP RAP: Automatically add row to table when another...
entry.content: I know this might seem like a pointless comment, given you have referenced the actual help, but is this really the only way of doing it? In the SM30 days, all that was required for automatic updating of language-dependent texts was a foreign key field type of "Key fields of a text table", and everything else was taken care of by the generated table maintenance. It seems a rather retrograde step to not have the framework deal generically with language dependent cases, rather having 100+ lines of code that will get copied and appropriately modified for each RAP application.

entry.title: Authorization Request for Sales BOM Access
entry.content:
Dear Team,

As BOM masking functionality is not available in SAP Public Cloud, we request an authorization-based restriction for BOM access.


Please can you outline how to maintain a BOM Role for the BOM Maintenance apps like Maintain Bill of Material to allow only users view / change Sales BOM (BOM Usage 5).

 

  • No access to Production BOM (Usage 1)
  • No access to Engineering/Plant BOM

 

Kindly create a business role/catalog with restriction on BOM Usage = 5 (Sales BOM only).

 


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Authorization Request for Sales BOM Access
entry.content:
In S/4HANA Public Cloud the Business catalog SAP_PLM_BC_MBOM_PC (Product Lifecycle Management - Material BOM ) has the restriction 'BOM Usage'

 

Here you can maintain a usage like 5 for Sales for Write , Read , Value Help and assign the role to a user so that they will only see BOMs 

( and create or change ) with usage 5

 

The restriction 'Authorization Group for BOM Usage' does not need to be maintained

entry.title: Re: Recurring invoice workflow option to post
entry.content:
In S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, you achieve this by combining the Manage Recurring Supplier Invoices app with Flexible Workflow for Supplier Invoices. That way, every recurring invoice is generated automatically but routed through approval before posting.

Follow the below recommended steps

  • Define recurrence rules (monthly, quarterly, etc.).
  • Specify workflow conditions (amount thresholds, vendor groups).
  • Document approval hierarchy.
  • Finally, do integration testing for recurring invoice creation → workflow trigger → approval → posting.

entry.title: Re: sourcing
entry.content:
Hello @at71,

please see below two current courses regarding Sourcing & Procurement:

  • SAP Certified - Implementation Consultant - SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, Sourcing and Procurement
  • SAP Certified - SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition, Sourcing and Procurement

Both contain a Learning Journey in which you find all input that you need before attempting a certification.

For both of them, SAP also offers "Get Certified"- Sessions in which you can recap the contents with a trainer.

Best regards & good luck,

Kerisha

entry.title: Re: Exchange Rate Type Configuration in SAP Public Cloud
entry.content:
Dear @Claudia234,

Yes. Changing the exchange rate type assigned to plan category PLN is a supported configuration change in Public Cloud. SAP states that configuration activities are built so that quarterly configuration upgrades do not override customer changes. SAP also documents that the exchange rate type is assigned to the plan category in configuration so changing PLN from P to M is standard configuration and the system will use the exchange rate type maintained for that category

More details below:

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/b249d650b15e4b3d9fc2077ee921abd0/427d9605efa54a7cb8d89abf0a9b4b02.html

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/1cbcff7ccd35405ab445b223c1ab1588/ba5d0f584ee39544e10000000a4450e5.html

The recommended next step is to regression-test the affected costing/planning scenarios in the test tenant before transport to production because SAP upgrade guidance includes regression testing as part of the upgrade validation approach.

More details below:

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/b249d650b15e4b3d9fc2077ee921abd0/90e9f061ea7349339af5c5d11a5c5de7.html

Summary:

The change from exchange rate type P to M for plan category PLN is a supported SSCUI change and should not be overwritten by future Public Cloud upgrades. Before moving it to production the impacted costing/planning processes using PLN should be validated, because after the change they will use M instead of P

Best Regards,

Dawid

entry.title: Re: Address does not exist error while checking delivery...
entry.content:
The error you are seeing “Address doesn’t exist (address number) …” happens when the purchase order item is still pointing to an address number that has been deleted in the Business Partner master data. Since the PO stores the address number as a reference, if that address record is removed, the PO cannot display or update the delivery address tab.

This is not a bug in ME22N; it is a data inconsistency caused by deleting the address record. The PO still points to the old number. The supported fix is to either:

  • assign a new delivery address directly in the PO, or
  • recreate the missing address in the Business Partner master and re‑link it.

entry.title: Re: Usage of ABAP Test Cockpit (ATC) in the cloud for on-premise developments
entry.content:
Hello @BjoernJueliger  @OlgaDolinskaja 


I found the solution.

The RFC function module OSL_GET_REMOTE_OBJECTS needs to be exposed in your Cloud Connector.

Thanks and regards,

Gerardo.

entry.title: MDK 25.9: Format Rule Not Applying Correctly
entry.content:
Hello Everyone,

I'm encountering an issue with my MDK Android application (built with MDK 25.9 and running on Android 14) where my formatrule is not applying as expected. The rule is designed to clean phone number inputs by removing non-digit characters and optionally limiting to 10 digits.

Here's the JavaScript code for the one of format rule PhoneNumberFormat rule:

JavaScript
 
/**
 * Describes this function...
 * @param {Icontext} context
 */
export default function PhoneNumberFormat(context) {
    // Get the current value of the control
    let value = context.getValue();

    // If input value is falsy (e.g., if the user deletes the input), then just return
    if (!value) return value;

    // Remove all non-digit characters
    value = value.replace(/\D/g, '');

    // Limit to 10 digits (optional, adjust as needed)
    if (value.length > 10) {
        value = value.substring(0, 10);
    }

    return value;
}

When testing on an Android 14 device, the input field does not seem to respect this formatting; non-numeric characters are not being removed, and the length restriction is not enforced visually or upon input.

I have already verified the following:

  • Rule Definition: The PhoneNumberFormat rule is correctly defined and present in the MDK metadata.

  • Target Control: The rule is properly assigned to the FormatRule property of the relevant SimpleProperty .

  • Data Binding: The control's Value property is correctly bound to a data source.

  • MDK Client Version: I'm using an MDK client built with MDK SDK version 25.9.

  • Android Version: The issue is observed on devices running Android 14.

Could you please provide insight into any known compatibility issues with format rules on MDK 25.9 running on Android 14, especially concerning string manipulation or input validation? Additionally, are there any specific debugging steps or workarounds you'd recommend for this scenario?

entry.title: Re: SmartRecruiters SmartMessaging
entry.content: SmartMessaging is called Candidate Messaging in the SAP SKU and this is included with the main SKU for SR.

entry.title: Join the upcoming What’s New in SAP Digital Manufacturing 2605 release webinars
entry.content:
Join our upcoming What's New in SAP Digital Manufacturing 2605 release webinars! 

With release 2605, the focus is on Worker Experience area, bringing improvements that directly impact how operators interact with systems on the shop floor and how production processes are executed and documented.

To give you a clear and practical overview, we are hosting two focused “What’s New” sessions, where we walk you through the latest innovations of the upcoming release and what they mean in practice.

Why join?

Apart from going through release notes and documentation on your own, these sessions are designed to help you quickly understand what is changing in the upcoming release and how it fits into your existing SAP Digital Manufacturing landscape. All in a concise, easy-to-digest format with a live Q&A as part of both sessions. 

Session 1: Worker Experience - Production Operator Dashboard 2.0 (beta)

📅April 15, 2026
⏰3:00 PM CEST

The Production Operator Dashboard (POD) 2.0 introduces a new approach to the operator experience.

In this session, we will cover:

  • The overall strategy and scope of POD 2.0
  • How it fits into the broader worker experience concept
  • UI design principles and integration with the process engine
  • Current limitations and what’s coming next

This session is particularly relevant if you are working with shop floor processes and want to understand how operator interaction is evolving.

Session 2: Worker Experience - Electronic Batch Record (EBR) & Electronic Device History Record (eDHR)

📅April 16, 2026
⏰3:00 PM CEST

The second session focuses on the latest capabilities and enhancements in:

  • Electronic Batch Records (EBR)
  • Electronic Device History Records (eDHR)

Who should attend?

These sessions are relevant for:

  • SAP Digital Manufacturing customers and partners
  • Manufacturing and shop floor experts
  • Solution architects and implementation teams
  • Anyone interested in the latest innovations in SAP DM

📝Register here. 

We look forward to having you join us!

 

Feedback or questions? 

💡We continuously evolve our enablement offerings based on feedback from customers and partners. If you have questions, are looking for specific resources, or would like to suggest topics for future webcasts, feel free to reach out to us at: engage-manufacturing@sap.com.  We’d be happy to hear your thoughts and continuously improve our content based on your input.

entry.title: Re: ABAP test cockpit (ATC) recommendations for governance of clean core ABAP development
entry.content:
Hi @lpodile,

in the parameters of the "Usage of APIs" check you will see, that released APIs are consumed from the checked (your) system. By the way, you can also anytime create your custom variant and provide the "newest" released APIs data source (e.g. check against the released APIs of the higher release as your system).

Kind Regards,
Olga.

entry.title: Re: AI Developer Challenge - Week 1: Grounding with SAP Generative AI Hub
entry.content: Week 1 Challenge Completed!!

entry.title: Re: Default Item Contact Email
entry.content: @louis_oli4, based on my understanding of the situation and system features, there wouldn’t be a way to set a default value. For classes and items, mass addition/update could be tried via connector. Previously, an enhancement was requested for item > class inheritance (including the “Contact Email” field), but it was closed by Learning Product Management due to lack of votes. There’s currently an open enhancement request to improve bulk upload of this field through the UI.

entry.title: Stay Connected with the HANA Community: Join Us at HANA Tech Con 2026!
entry.content:
HANA Tech Con 2026, part of the Code Connect event series, brings together developers, users, and partners who are passionate about HANA. More than just a technical event, HANA Tech Con focuses on collaboration and shared learning. Participants will have the chance to connect with experts and peers from around the world, discover new perspectives, and explore potential partnerships within the vibrant HANA ecosystem.

 

Learn from Experts and Real-World Experiences

Industry experts will share their experience through technical sessions, and interactive demonstrations. These sessions highlight the latest HANA features, modern development approaches, and proven best practices.

Attendees will also get the chance to explore real-world use cases and see how HANA technologies are implemented in practice.

Support the Event as a Sponsor

Supporting the event offers a great opportunity to engage directly with a highly focused community of developers, partners, and SAP users.

Sponsors benefit from strong visibility throughout the event, including brand presence in key areas of the venue and recognition during major sessions. Companies can also promote their solutions through event communication channels and interact directly with attendees who are interested in HANA technologies.

For those who want to showcase their products or services, an optional booth space can be included as part of the sponsorship package.

Check all the benefits and sponsorship options here: LINK

Registration will be opening soon!

Mark your calendar: Registration opens on April 16, 2026.

Tickets are free, so make sure to secure your spot as soon as registration goes live: they tend to go fast!

 

Highlights from Last Year

‌👉‌ Follow the event on LinkedIn to stay up to date with the
latest news on speakers, sessions, and announcements for HANA Tech Con 2026: LINK

Stay Connected, and see you at HANA Tech Con 2026!

entry.title: Re: Issue in Generating Intercompany Billing Request – Er...
entry.content: Hey Dawid yes, I used standard data but it doesn't work also , I dont know whats am missing , did you create master data for your client like material ,I am wondering also if its related with pricing , thank you again

entry.title: Re: CO07 Without material order creation
entry.content: Use CO07 with an appropriate order type, describe the tool being produced, assign components and operations manually, and release the order. This way, you can track costs and production without needing a material master.

entry.title: Re: Note 2267878 - Parameter Effectivity has been hidden...
entry.content:
The simplification item you quoted (SAP Note 2267878) is essentially telling you that “parameter effectivity” in classification is no longer available in S/4HANA. What that means is in S/4HANA, this functionality has been hidden/removed. SAP recommends using Variant Configuration with object dependencies instead.

Having said that, the key point is no mandatory technical steps are required before migration. The only activity is to analyze your ECC usage of parameter effectivity and, if relevant, remodel it in VC before or after migration.

In short, if you never used parameter effectivity, you can proceed without changes. If you did, you must redesign those rules in Variant Configuration, because parameter effectivity will not exist in S/4HANA.

entry.title: Re: Production Orders without Planned Orders
entry.content: SAP doesn’t deliver a single report for this, but you can achieve it by joining AFKO/AFPO (production orders) with PLAF (planned orders) and filtering for FERT materials. That way you’ll get exactly the list you need.
